# Provider Privacy Checklist: Real Provider Configuration

**Purpose**: Validate provider, privacy, remote-confirmation, and redaction requirements quality before task generation
**Created**: 2026-07-07
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are provider default-mode requirements fully specified for both absent and invalid provider configuration? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-002]
- [x] CHK002 Are local real-provider requirements complete enough to distinguish local/self-hosted use from managed remote use? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003, Plan §Technical Context]
- [x] CHK003 Are request compatibility requirements complete for existing direct-text and workspace-file callers? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004]
- [x] CHK004 Are provider payload boundaries fully specified for included and excluded fields? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005, Spec §FR-006]
- [x] CHK005 Are remote-provider requirements complete for configuration, allowlisting, per-request confirmation, and denial when absent? [Completeness, Spec §FR-007]
- [x] CHK006 Are secret-blocking requirements specified for all non-local provider paths before provider contact? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008]
- [x] CHK007 Are response-validation requirements complete for malformed, empty, mismatched, non-textual, and oversized provider responses? [Completeness, Spec §FR-010]
- [x] CHK008 Are redaction requirements complete across user-visible errors, diagnostics, CLI stderr, MCP output, Zed diagnostics, and evidence? [Completeness, Spec §FR-012, Spec §SC-006]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK009 Is "local to the user's machine" defined with objective locality criteria? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003, Plan §Constraints]
- [x] CHK010 Is "explicitly configured" defined clearly enough to avoid accidental activation from arbitrary request content? [Clarity, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-002]
- [x] CHK011 Is "explicit remote confirmation" defined as request-scoped rather than session- or provider-scoped? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007]
- [x] CHK012 Is "obvious secret pattern" sufficiently bounded by existing authoritative secret-detection rules? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK013 Are normalized provider failure categories named clearly enough to drive task decomposition? [Clarity, Spec §FR-011]
- [x] CHK014 Is the no-mutation boundary stated unambiguously for source files and editor buffers? [Clarity, Spec §FR-013]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK015 Do the spec and plan consistently preserve mock/offline as default? [Consistency, Spec §FR-001, Plan §Summary]
- [x] CHK016 Do remote-confirmation requirements align between spec, data model, and provider-configuration contract? [Consistency, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-007, Contracts]
- [x] CHK017 Do provider payload exclusions align with the constitution's provider boundary and log-redaction rules? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006, Constitution II]
- [x] CHK018 Do the stated limits in spec, plan, and provider contract use the same input, segment, output, and timeout values? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009, Plan §Performance Goals, Contracts]
- [x] CHK019 Do Zed wrapper configuration requirements remain consistent with the documented D064 inherited-environment limitation? [Consistency, Plan §Constraints, Decisions D064]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK020 Are success criteria measurable for default mock preservation, local-provider success, remote denial, secret blocking, failure mapping, redaction, no-mutation, and provider payload inspection? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001-SC-009]
- [x] CHK021 Are local-provider success criteria separated from optional manual remote smoke validation? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-002, Research]
- [x] CHK022 Are provider failure success criteria specific enough to generate tests for timeout, unavailability, throttling, rejection, malformed response, and oversized output? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-005]
- [x] CHK023 Are redaction success criteria objectively verifiable without storing sensitive sample content? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-006]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK024 Are primary local-provider scenarios covered for both direct text and allowed workspace files? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1]
- [x] CHK025 Are alternate remote-provider scenarios covered for unconfirmed, confirmed, and confirmed-with-secret cases? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 2]
- [x] CHK026 Are provider failure scenarios covered for unavailable, timeout, and invalid response behavior? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 3]
- [x] CHK027 Are compatibility scenarios covered for existing callers that omit any new remote-confirmation field? [Coverage, Spec §FR-004]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK028 Are unsafe provider-target edge cases specified, including malformed targets, unsafe schemes, embedded credentials, and unsupported hosts? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Data Model §ProviderTarget]
- [x] CHK029 Are provider diagnostics edge cases specified for accidental source text, translation text, headers, tokens, paths, and raw response bodies? [Edge Case, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-012]
- [x] CHK030 Are response-size and segment-count boundary cases defined before task generation? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-010]
- [x] CHK031 Are unsupported input and path-safety edge cases retained from previous features rather than reopened ambiguously? [Edge Case, Spec §Assumptions]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK032 Are external provider lifecycle assumptions documented as prerequisites rather than host-install tasks? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Target Platform]
- [x] CHK033 Are dependency decisions for the HTTP client explicit enough for supply-chain review before implementation? [Dependency, Plan §Primary Dependencies, Decisions D069]
- [x] CHK034 Are publication, custom Zed UI, paid provider setup, and automatic replacement explicitly out of scope across spec and plan? [Scope, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Scale/Scope]

## Notes

- Focus areas selected: provider configuration, remote privacy gate, response validation, redaction, host-footprint boundaries.
- Depth level: Standard reviewer checklist before task generation.
- Actor/timing: PR/spec reviewer before `/speckit-tasks`.
